description:
Goal: activation input widening should be owned by the fact graph, not by ActivationMap or CheckSemanticClosure. Signal: AnalyzeActivation contributes callee input facts, Activation input is the refine_widen join of caller contributions, CheckSemanticClosure no longer walks and mutates the frontier, and the quicksort trace-summary shows the double activation gone. Strategy: move callee activation contributions into AnalyzeActivation outputs, make Activation input a multi-owner Inputs fact, have activation analysis subscribe to that joined input, and reduce closure checking to a read-only predicate over activation/return/analyzed facts.

Change:
- Replace world.activate side-store widening with caller-owned Activation input contributions.
- SeedRoot contributes the root entry activation input.
- AnalyzeActivation contributes callee activation inputs and executable needs from each resolved callsite.
- CheckSemanticClosure reads the live root activation facts and completion facts; it does not call activate or rediscover callees by BFS.
- Delete ActivationMap::activate widening, activation_revisions bookkeeping, closure BFS, and local output dedupe built around max revisions.

Why:
- The caller already knows each callee and argument vector. The activation input is physically the join of those caller observations, so the work graph should own the join.

Checks:
- Quicksort first compile has no double activation and no dead transient activations outside the final closure.
- Redefining main from qsort to foo retracts only the actual dependent activation chain.
- Quicksort and Enum.reduce closed frontiers remain semantically correct.
title: Compiler2 activations: make input a caller-contributed fact
